description_long: This is a classic wooden barrel with a cylindrical, bulging shape, constructed from vertical wooden staves held together by six wide, metallic hoops. The wood has a light, natural finish with visible grain patterns, and the metal bands are a dull gray, suggesting galvanized steel or iron. The top is open, revealing the hollow interior, and there is a small circular bunghole on one side, typically used for filling or emptying the barrel. The surface of the wood appears slightly textured, while the metal bands are smoother but matte. The overall craftsmanship suggests durability and traditional cooperage methods. This type of barrel is commonly found in urban settings for storage, decoration, or as a receptacle for waste or rainwater.
description: A traditional wooden barrel with light wood staves and six gray metal hoops, open at the top and featuring a side bunghole. The surface is textured wood with matte metal bands, giving it a sturdy, rustic appearance.
description_view_0: Front view showing the open top, wood grain, metal hoops, and a visible bunghole on the side.
description_view_1: Side view with the open top and all six metal hoops visible, bunghole partially visible on the left.
description_view_2: Rear view with the open top and uninterrupted wood staves, no bunghole visible.
description_view_3: Opposite side view with the open top and metal hoops, no bunghole visible.
